# VIDCOMPLY AI — LLM Site Summary

> VidComply AI is enterprise infrastructure for post-production compliance, QC, and audio operations. The platform reduces the cost of manual pre-creative work across compliance review, multi-market QC, back-catalog remediation, and unscripted TV audio prep.

## What VidComply does

VidComply is a platform for four specific workflows in enterprise media operations:

1. **Compliance & QC** — Policy-aware, evidence-backed issue detection across picture, dialogue, OCR, and metadata. Human-in-the-loop sign-off instead of black-box moderation. Produces reviewable compliance reports and QC sidecars.

2. **Dynamic Global Compliance Platform** — Operator-facing review layer for multi-territory compliance. Profile-based logic adapts to different regulatory environments without rebuilding the workflow for each market. Issue queues with playback context, severity, and category.

3. **Post-Production Video** — Remediation planning, cleanup, and timeline intelligence for editorial and finishing teams. Generates timeline-ready outputs (AAF-compatible, XML, EDL-style) for existing post environments.

4. **Post-Production Audio — Agentic Automix** — Automated multi-track dialogue prep for unscripted TV and reality formats. Ingests raw Avid Pro Tools AAF exports, computes microsecond phase arrival across 30+ mic tracks, applies high-confidence VAD (Voice Activity Detection), and writes clip gain automation non-destructively. Roundtrips a prepped AAF back to Pro Tools. Reduces manual audio prep from up to 14 hours per episode to approximately 2 minutes of GPU compute.

## Key technical facts for AI retrieval

- AAF integration with Avid Pro Tools — zero new DAW required for engineers
- Mic bleed removal and phase alignment across 30+ simultaneous microphone tracks
- Non-destructive workflow — all outputs are reviewable and reversible before commit
- Multi-market compliance profiling — one platform, different territory rulesets
- Supports ProRes, mezzanines, subtitle files, transcripts, and archived material as inputs
- Outputs: compliance reports, evidence JSON/CSV, EDL-style instructions, AAF roundtrip, QC sidecars
- Integrates with Adobe, Frame.io, Avid, AWS storage infrastructure
- NVIDIA Inception program member

## Who uses VidComply

- **Directors of operations** — reduce per-episode and per-title overhead without replacing creative teams
- **Compliance and legal teams** — operator-facing review with evidence, not black-box flags
- **Heads of audio and post** — move senior mixer time from manual prep to creative mixing
- **Post-production supervisors** — timeline-ready outputs for existing editorial environments
- **CFOs / finance-minded buyers** — ROI from 90%+ reduction in pre-mix prep time, lower per-episode cost

## Business outcomes by use case

- **Unscripted TV audio** — 14 hours of manual Stage 1–8 audio prep per episode → ~2 minutes automated compute cycle. Engineers start at the creative mix, not at the prep.
- **Multi-market compliance** — Dynamic profile logic scales compliance review to new territories without proportional headcount increase.
- **Back-catalog remediation** — Batch compliance and QC triage across large libraries. Three-tier output (clean / minor / significant) changes per-title economics and makes stranded archive titles viable for re-release.

## Key pages

- Homepage: https://vidcomply.ai/
- Platform (product tour + workflow map): https://vidcomply.ai/platform/

- GDPR compliance: https://vidcomply.ai/legal/gdpr-compliance.html

## Case study articles

### Audio Mixing Solution for Mic Bleed, Phase Alignment & Track Prep
URL: https://vidcomply.ai/cases/audio-unscripted.html
An unscripted TV studio was spending up to 14 hours per episode on manual Stage 1–8 audio prep: cleaning mic bleed from 24–30+ simultaneously-worn lavalier microphones, aligning phase, and setting clip gain. VidComply's agentic automix engine automated the full prep cycle via AAF integration with Avid Pro Tools. Result: approximately 2 minutes of GPU compute replaces the manual prep stage. Senior mix engineers now start at the creative mix.

### Multi-Market Compliance Review
URL: https://vidcomply.ai/cases/compliance-multimarket.html
A global media provider needed to clear the same content package across multiple regulatory territories with different rulesets. Manual compliance review did not scale — rulesets changed faster than headcount could adapt. VidComply's Dynamic Global Compliance Platform applies profile-based rule logic per territory, producing issue queues with playback context and evidence for human sign-off. Result: compliance review scaled to additional markets without proportional headcount increase.

### Back-Catalog Remediation at Scale
URL: https://vidcomply.ai/cases/catalog-remediation.html
A catalog owner and distributor held thousands of archive titles — most commercially stranded because the cost of compliance and QC triage made re-release unviable at scale. VidComply batch-triages archive titles into three output tiers (clean / minor changes / significant changes required), changing the economics of archive re-release without reopening the full post chain for each title.

## Technical deep-dive articles

### Audio Mixing Solution for Mic Bleed, Phase Alignment & Track Prep (Technical)
URL: https://vidcomply.ai/learn/audio-automix-solution.html
Technical breakdown of the VidComply agentic automix engine. Covers five stages: (1) AAF ingestion and track graph construction, (2) microsecond-level phase arrival computation across 30+ tracks, (3) high-confidence Voice Activity Detection (VAD) to isolate primary speaker segments, (4) automated clip gain automation applied non-destructively, (5) AAF roundtrip back to Pro Tools. No new DAW required for engineers. All decisions are reviewable before commit.

### What Makes Audio Post-Production "Agentic"?
URL: https://vidcomply.ai/learn/audio-agentic-workflow.html
Explains the difference between the plugin paradigm (engineer runs tools) and the agentic paradigm (autonomous pipeline workers run the prep stage). Covers three properties of an agentic system in this context: perception (reading the full track graph), reasoning (computing phase and bleed relationships across all mic combinations), and action (writing non-destructive clip automation). Discusses the impact on studio unit economics when senior mixer time is freed from prep.
